meaning: adj.隐蔽的,偷偷摸摸的,[律]在丈夫保护下的n.掩蔽处,隐藏处
examples: As the individual matures, that overt talking to oneself becomes covert talking to oneself, but thinking still shows up as a laryngeal habit.
He stole a covert glance at her across the table.
Then he made his deliberations covert.
Now at 17, she has recently been appointed as an agent in the Covert Operations Division.
But approaching photography like a covert operation is not the most ethical way to get a great shot.
